question:
Suppose there are 500 items in the lot and their manufacturer claims
that no more than 5% are defective. Then when the shipment is received at an
assembly plant, a quality control manager asks his staff to take a random sample
of size 20 from it without replacement and will accept the lot if there are at most
1defective item in the sample. If let say 5% are defective in the entire lot, then it
consists of 25 defective items. What is the probability that the manager will accept
the lot?
answer:
Let X be the number of defective items in a sample of size 20, then it has
a hypergeometric distribution and it probability function is as follows
$$
P(X=x)=\frac{\binom{25}{x}\binom{475}{20-x}}{\binom{500}{20}} \quad x=0,1,2, \ldots, 20
$$


The probability that the manager will accept the lot, means that there will be at most 1 defective item in a random sample of size 20 , is as follows:

$$
\begin{aligned}
P(X \leq 1) & =\frac{\binom{25}{0}\binom{475}{20}}{\binom{500}{20}}+\frac{\binom{25}{1}\binom{475}{19}}{\binom{500}{20}} \\
& =0.3511941+0.3850813=0.7362754
\end{aligned}
$$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
